shower.thought: We spend the first part of our lives learning rules we spend the second part breaking.
Your phone knows more about you than you know about yourself, but it can't tell you who you are.
The only reason you remember most of your childhood is because other people keep reminding you it happened.
You've been a different person every seven years, but you call yourself the same name.
Embarrassment is just realizing that someone else's memory is now better than yours.
The worst part about keeping a secret isn't hiding it — it's knowing someone else knows and didn't tell you they know.
You can't actually imagine a new color, which means your mind is only ever remixing what it's already seen.
Most of your life is spent trying to get back to how you felt at a specific moment that wasn't even that good when it was happening.
You trust your friends with your secrets but not with your passwords, which is exactly backwards.
Every conversation ends with one person deciding it's over, and the other person just goes along with it.
Time moves faster the older you get because each year becomes a smaller percentage of your life — which means you're literally feeling math.
You can't know if someone is lying to you as much as you can know if they're telling you something they believe is true.
The worst decisions you've made were probably the most confident ones.
You're nostalgic for times when you weren't happy, just times when you were distracted.
Someone out there is having the worst day of their life while you're having an ordinary Tuesday.
We mourn people we never properly knew because we loved the version of them we invented.
Your body is replacing itself constantly, so technically you're already a different person — you're just too slow to notice.
You can't go home again because home isn't a place, it's a person you were.
The scariest monsters in movies are just people who stopped listening to the world telling them no.
Boredom is just your brain asking for something harder to do, and you usually respond by giving it something easier.

motivational.thought: The master has failed more times than the beginner has even tried. — Stephen McCranie
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you haven't made yet. — Unknown
What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
You are not lazy. You are just working toward the wrong things. — Unknown
The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek. — Joseph Campbell
Comparison is the thief of joy, but it's also the thief of your potential. — Unknown
Small progress is still progress. A step forward beats a thousand plans. — Unknown
Fear is just excitement without breath. — Fritz Perls
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Ancient Proverb
Your limitations are mostly just the edge of your effort. — Unknown
The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
We suffer more in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
Don't wait to be ready. Readiness is built in the doing. — Unknown
The world breaks everyone, and afterward, some are strong at the broken places. — Ernest Hemingway
What you resist persists. What you accept transforms. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. — Unknown
The mind is everything. What you think, you become. — Buddha
Bravery is not the absence of fear—it's moving forward anyway. — Unknown
Your willingness to look stupid is proportional to your capacity to learn. — Unknown
The only failure is the one you don't learn from. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she'd be perfect if she lost a few pounds.
Turns out she was already planning to—just in a different way.

My dad said he wanted to die peacefully in his sleep like his father did.
Not screaming in terror like the passengers in his car.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.

My therapist says I'm avoidant, so I stopped going.
She seemed upset when I explained this was working great for me.

I asked the suicide hotline if they had any openings.
Apparently that's not what they meant by "we're here for you."

My wife left me because I'm too pessimistic.
Which honestly just proves my point.

I told my friend he'd be a great father someday.
He said, "Thanks." I said, "I'm serious—keep the receipts."

My dog has separation anxiety, which is ironic because I have abandonment issues.
We're basically perfect for each other until I inevitably disappoint him.

I'm on a seafood diet.
I see food, I eat it, and then I see my reflection and lose my appetite.

My boss asked if I had any weaknesses.
I said, "I'm too honest." He said, "I don't think that's a weakness."
I said, "I don't care what you think."

I used to play Russian roulette, but I kept losing.
Now I just work in customer service—same odds, better pay.

My friend said he was going to get in shape.
Last I heard, he's doing great—round is technically a shape.

I told my kids that Santa wasn't real.
Turns out my ex-wife had already told them that about me.

My lawyer says I have a strong case.
Unfortunately, it's a case of poor judgment spread across multiple incidents.

I went to a self-help seminar called "How to Stop Being Codependent."
My wife said I shouldn't go, so I didn't. See? I'm already better.

I asked my doctor if I'd live to see a hundred.
He said, "Probably not—based on how you drive."

My therapist asked what my biggest fear was.
I said, "That one day I'll realize I wasted all this time talking to you."
She said, "We still have fifty minutes left."

I'm trying to be more positive, so I've started a gratitude journal.
Yesterday I was grateful I didn't have to go to work.
Today I'm grateful for that.

My friend said he was cutting out toxic people from his life.
I asked, "Starting with yourself?"
He stopped texting me after that.

I told my boss I was having a existential crisis.
He said, "That's great—can you have it after 5 PM?"
